title: Sets (Uniques) 🔮
description: A collection that automatically removes duplicates.

slides.title: No Duplicates Allowed! 👮
slides.content:
# The Exclusive Club

A **Set** is very similar to a List, but with one massive difference:
**A Set can NEVER contain duplicate values.**

If you try to add 'Apple' to a Set that already contains 'Apple', it simply ignores it!

This is incredibly useful for keeping track of things like 'Unique Visitors' to a website.

slides.title: Creating a Set {}
slides.content:
## Curly Braces

While Lists use `[]`, Sets use curly braces `{}`.

```dart
Set<String> colors = {'Red', 'Green', 'Blue'};

colors.add('Yellow'); // Works perfectly!
colors.add('Red'); // Completely ignored, 'Red' is already there.

print(colors);
```

quizData.question: What is the primary difference between a List and a Set?

options.text: A Set can hold infinite data.
A Set automatically discards duplicate entries.
A Set is used exclusively for numbers.

quizData.explanation: Sets are unordered collections of unique items. Duplicates are silently ignored.

slides.title: Unordered Chaos 🎲
slides.content:
## No Indexes Here

Because a Set focuses on being unique, it completely discards the concept of "order".

You **cannot** ask a Set for item `[0]` or `[1]`. There is no front or back of the line!

You can only check if an item *exists* using `.contains()`:
`print(colors.contains('Red')); // true`

quizData.question:
Fill in the blank to check if 'Admin' is inside the users Set:
bool hasAdmin = users.____('Admin');

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: Watch how the duplicate 'Mario' naturally vanishes!
